title: How rehearsed was the first meeting of Shinji and Gendo
display_title: How rehearsed was the first meeting of Shinji and Gendo
wikitext:
[]: How rehearsed was the first meeting of Shinji and Gendo?             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 7:27 pm We see in the first episode how Gendo (arguably) put up a manipulative scheme with bandaged Rei to lure Shinji into the Eva out of guilt... Do you reckon Fuyutsuki, Ritsuko and/or Misato knew and were into that? It's not hard to imagine Gendo saying "so, if he refuses, I call you Fuyutsuki, you put Rei on the phone and you Ritsuko, bring her in through the hangar, even though there (probably) is a direct route from the hospital to the Entry Plug" I would bet Ritsuko and Fuyutsiki knew and played a part in Gendo's (alleged) psychological manipulation; Misato maybe not, but seeing where that was going she started to push Shinji into the Eva too... What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Nov 30, 2013 7:35 pm That all depends on whether you think Gendo was following an elaborate plan to mentally break Shinji, that he genuinely didn't care about Shinji and was ready to go to Plan B (Shoving Rei's listless broken body in the Eva) for pure convenience, or his behavior towards Shinji is unconsciously motivated by malice. Bringing Rei out was probably a ploy to guilt Shinji into piloting but I doubt he had it planned to that level. Gendo's reputation as a master manipulator tends to be exaggerated as I see it. It was really the only thing he could do in any case. If anything, he was offering Shinji a chance to not be a douche and make the hurt little girl pilot the robot instead of him. Shinji was being petulant, even though Gendo is plenty guilty of being a neglectful dick. Understandably petulant. The entire situation is insane. Especially when dealing with a teenager, personalizing a problem can be effective in persuading someone to action. "This specific person will suffer" is a lot more effective a proposition than "everyone will die". He doesn't know everyone but he sees the pretty girl that looks like his mom right in front of him.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Nov 30, 2013 8:08 pm It depends if you're talking about the original TV series or Rebuild : In NGE : He probably didn't planed it far in advance, since Shinji wasn't even supposed to get into the Eva program, but had to be called in Tokyo-3 because Rei was injured, and Sachiel happened to attack the same day. It's possible that he called for Rei to be sent to EVA-01's cage as a last effort to convince Shinji to pilot, and if he still refuses, well Rei has to pilot anyway and she would be right here. SO I don't think that Fuyutsuki or Ritsuko played any part in a plan to manipulate Shinji, since there doesn't seems to be a plan to begin with, just Gendo calling for Rei when Shinji refused, and maybe thinking that with some chance that would made him change his mind(which it did) In Rebuild : He probably planned it since Rei was injured : here EVA-01 was meant to be Shinji's Eva since the very beginning while Rei was EVA-00's pilot since day one too(unlike in NGE where she was EVA-01's assigned pilot and got to pilot the prototype because Shinji's synch-rate with Unit 01 is better than hers). Also, being completely clueless about his "destiny" as an Eva pilot until the last minute was also part of Gendo's plan to manipulate him into ultimately awakening EVA-01, so it's very possible that he planned various scenarios to make him pilot the fucking robot, parading Rei as a guilt-trip being one of them. Here it's also possible that Ritsuko and Fuyutsuki where in cahoot with him.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Nov 30, 2013 8:26 pm Well, since it's the NGE main thread, then we talk about NGE I find hard to believe that with less that 3 days for planning Gendo hadn't concocted a plan to insure Shinji got into the Eva, after all, that's all they needed...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 02, 2013 12:31 pm As for Rebuild, there are many things on which we haven't gotten the fu Gendo does manipulate Shinji, but always indirectly with the long arm and the slight nudges that make everything else happen by itself; If he really was as depraved as this, all he'd need to say is some variation of, "If you get into the robot and do what I want, I will love you forever", and that's all it takes. Shinji would do anything short of deliberate killing for that end, he's rather hopeless in that regard. Consider episode 4, where he nearly lets the only available pilot waltz out of the city without comment, probably for reasons similar to those Misato is shown struggling with. He probably expected him to come back, but that's no guarantee. It's not that he morally wouldnt resort to it, but that Gendo is far too clumsy at handling anything Shinji related to do such a thing directly and openly instead through middle men (and women) he has a much firmer grip on. EoE makes it rather clear that he feels helpless before the kid.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Dec 02, 2013 1:57 pm ^ I think it's more that if Gendo says out loud "If you get into the robot and do what I want, I will love you forever" or something similar, Shinji will simply not buy it, remember that he abandoned him when he was still a toddler, just after his mother died no less! On a practical level Gendo has to be subtle if he doesn't want his son to ragequit. Besides if you look closely, be it in NGE or Rebuild, Gendo never lied to Shinji : when he asked if he called him because he needed him, Gendo answered by the affirmative, which is the truth, whe he said "Good work, Shinji.", that was the truth : he did well(very well), when he asked him why he activated the Dummy System, he answered that it was because the enemy had to be destroyed and that he didn't wanted to do it, and so would had died, which are true! Even in Q he didn't lied to him : "That's Evangelion 13, an unit for you and that pilot, when the time come, pilot this Eva with him.", not a single lie here. The main difference being that by this time he seems to have accepted that he'll have to destroy his son to reach his ultimate goal, and did it ruthlessly, and even then he never lied to Shinji, even via Fuyutsuki as a proxy(so far everything he revealed to Shinji in that party of Shogi seems to be the truth) Also, as you said, he's actually wary of his son and think that he can't do anything but to harm him without Yui's guidance(which, following the events of the series and worst, in Q, is also true!)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 02, 2013 6:23 pm ElMariachi wrote: Shinji will simply not buy it
